insight: Codify minimum viable input schema and auto-reject analysis requests that fail completeness checks.
Install mandatory input-contract validator with completeness score; deny verdicts below acceptance threshold.
Require a strict analysis payload schema and return explicit missing-field diagnostics before producing regime outputs.
Adopt mandatory pre-analysis checklist and schema validation gate; block outputs when critical fields are absent.
Enforce hard input-schema validation before analysis; return missing-field diagnostics when incomplete.
Implement mandatory preflight schema gate with explicit missing-field report; block analysis output below completeness threshold.
Formalize required-input contract and auto-reject incomplete analysis requests with structured remediation checklist.
Introduce strict input completeness scoring with hard fail below threshold; return machine-readable missing-input diagnostics.
Default doctrine: if evidence package is incomplete, reject deployment and require a full net-of-cost, regime-segmented validation before reconsideration.
When a gate-adjust variant remains below PF 1.0 with >200 trades, priority should shift from further gate micro-tuning to entry selectivity and trade lifecycle control (time-stop + staged exits).
If PF remains <1.0 after a gate-adjust iteration on >200 trades, prioritize signal quality and exit efficiency over additional gate complexity.
If PF is ~1.00 on large samples (>300 trades), prioritize drawdown compression and trade-quality reduction before any further complexity changes.
